prompt: You are visiting London for the first time. You want to go from your hotel to the British Museum. Please explain how you would get there and why you choose that way.
reference_answer:
10	I would definitely take the Tube to get to the British Museum. It is the most efficient and iconic way to travel in London. I choose this method because it completely avoids the unpredictable above-ground traffic, and the underground map is incredibly straightforward for tourists to navigate.|For my first time in London, I would use the London Underground, commonly known as the Tube. It's highly convenient and fast. I prefer this route because the stations are well-marked, and it saves a lot of time compared to sitting in a taxi during rush hour.
9	I would take the Tube to go to the British Museum. It is very fast and easy to use. I choose the Tube because I want to avoid traffic jams on the streets, and taking the subway is a classic London experience.|I will take a classic red double-decker bus. I choose the bus because it is cheaper than a taxi, and I can enjoy the beautiful city view of London on the second floor while traveling to the museum.
8	I think I will take the underground. It is very convenient for tourists. I choose this way because it is fast and I won't get stuck in traffic.|I want to take a bus to the British Museum. Because the red bus is very famous in London, and I can see the city from the window.
7	I will take the Tube to the museum. Because it is very fast and cheap.|I choose the bus. The bus is red and beautiful. I can see the city.
6	Take the Tube. It is fast and good.|I go by bus. Because I like to look outside.
5	Take subway. Because very fast.|I want bus. Red bus good.
4	Go by bus. See city.|Tube. Fast.
3	Take car. Museum.|Bus. Go there.
2	Go.|Museum.|Train.

prompt: Look at the picture of a London double-decker bus route. Describe what you see and what information is important for tourists.
reference_answer:
10	In the picture, I see a classic red double-decker bus displaying its route number and final destination clearly on the front electronic board. For tourists, the most vital pieces of information are the route number to ensure they are on the correct path, the final destination to avoid going the wrong way, and the list of major stops printed on the side of the bus.|I am looking at a traditional London red bus. The image shows the bus route number at the top and the destination it's heading towards. For a tourist, paying attention to the route number and the key landmarks written on the side panel is crucial to making sure they reach their desired attractions safely.

prompt: Some people prefer taking a taxi in London, while others prefer public transportation. Which one do you prefer? Explain your reasons.
reference_answer:
10	I strongly prefer taking public transportation in London, specifically the Tube and double-decker buses. While the iconic black cabs are comfortable and offer personalized service, public transport is significantly more cost-effective and often faster during rush hour. Furthermore, riding public transport allows you to experience the city like a true local.|If I had to choose, I would prefer taking a black cab. Although it is more expensive, London cab drivers must pass 'The Knowledge' test, meaning they know every street perfectly. It offers a private, comfortable ride and drops you off right at your exact destination, which is perfect when carrying heavy luggage.
9	I prefer public transportation like the Tube. It is much cheaper than taking a taxi, and you don't have to worry about traffic jams. Also, the Tube network covers almost everywhere in London, making it very convenient for traveling around the city.
8	I like taking public transportation. It is cheap and fast. Taxis are too expensive for me. Also, taking the bus or subway is very easy in London.
7	I prefer public transport. It is cheap. Taxis are too much money. The Tube is very fast.
6	I like Tube. It is cheap and fast. Taxi is expensive.
5	Public transport. Because cheap. No traffic.
4	Take bus. Cheap. Taxi expensive.
3	Bus is good. Money.
2	Bus.|Taxi.

prompt: You watched a short video about Big Ben and Westminster. Please summarize the key points for a friend who has never been to the UK.
reference_answer:
10	Westminster is essentially the political and historical heart of London. The video highlighted the iconic Big Ben, pointing out that 'Big Ben' is actually the name of the massive bell inside, not the tower itself. It also showcased the magnificent Houses of Parliament right next to the River Thames. It's an absolute must-visit area for its breathtaking Gothic architecture and rich British history.|To summarize, the video introduced the Westminster area, which is home to the UK government. The main attraction is the Elizabeth Tower, famous for the giant bell called Big Ben. It sits along the River Thames alongside the Houses of Parliament, representing the core of British politics and classic architecture.
9	The video is about Westminster, the political center of the UK. It showed the famous Big Ben, which is actually the bell inside the clock tower. It also showed the Houses of Parliament next to the river. It is a very beautiful and historical place you must see.
8	Westminster is a very famous place in London. The video showed Big Ben, which is a big clock tower, but the name is for the bell inside. You can also see the parliament building there near the river.

6	It is about Big Ben. Big Ben is a big clock. It is in Westminster. Very famous place.

prompt: Describe a memorable sightseeing experience you would like to have in London. Please organize your answer with a clear beginning, middle, and ending.
reference_answer:
10	To begin my ideal sightseeing day in London, I would start with a morning flight on the London Eye to get a breathtaking panoramic view of the entire city. In the middle of the day, I would take a leisurely stroll along the River Thames, stopping by the Tate Modern to appreciate some contemporary art and grabbing lunch at Borough Market. Finally, my day would end in the West End, watching a world-class musical, making it a truly unforgettable cultural experience.|For my dream London trip, I would first visit the Tower of London in the morning to see the Crown Jewels and learn about the city's dark history. After that, I would spend my afternoon enjoying a traditional British afternoon tea at a fancy hotel like The Ritz. To conclude the day, I would walk around Covent Garden to enjoy the street performers and the lively evening atmosphere.
9	First, I would like to visit the British Museum in the morning to see the Rosetta Stone and mummies. Then, in the afternoon, I would go to Hyde Park to relax and have a picnic on the grass. Finally, I would end my day by watching a beautiful sunset near the London Bridge.
8	In the beginning, I want to go to the London Eye to see the city view. Then, I will eat fish and chips for lunch. In the end, I will go shopping in Oxford Street and buy some souvenirs for my friends.
7	First, I go to Big Ben to take photos. Then I eat traditional British food for lunch. Finally, I go shopping. It will be a good day.
6	First go to London Eye. Then eat lunch. Finally go to shopping.
5	Go to museum first. Then eat food. Go home.
4	See Big Ben. Eat. Sleep.
3	Play. Eat food. Good.
